summary: The AFI commitment plane on BASE is implemented solely in afi-token/src (three Foundry/OZ contracts); afi-mint's contracts/ are empty stubs and afi-xerc20 is a vendored third-party bridge fork with no AFI surface. On-chain the protocol persists only ERC-20 emissions balances/totalSupply (86B hard cap), ERC-1155 receipt balances, AccessControl role assignments, and immutable coordinator wiring; the signal/epoch provenance link (signalId, epochId, beneficiary, amounts) exists only as emitted event-log topics (MintCoordinated/EmissionsMinted/ReceiptMinted), never as contract storage. No content hash, payload hash, score, validator id, UWR axes, or ruleset version is anchored anywhere on-chain, and minting is gated only by EMISSIONS_ROLE (granted in the mainnet Pattern A to a single Treasury Safe). A third party therefore can confirm that a mint happened and stayed under the cap, but cannot verify from chain data + published rules that the mint was legitimately scored or bound to real off-chain evidence — replay/determinism lives entirely off-chain in afi-mint/afi-reactor. This is the current breadcrumb anchor vs the docs' aspirational "codex-replayable" immutable commitment layer.

answers.question: C1: What is stored on-chain today — enumerate every Solidity event, struct, field, role, and cap in the commitment layer.
answers.answer: The on-chain commitment plane is three contracts in afi-token/src. EVENTS: AFIToken.EmissionsMinted(address indexed beneficiary, uint256 amount); AFIToken.AdminUpdated(address indexed previousAdmin, address indexed newAdmin); AFIToken.EmissionsControllerUpdated(address indexed previousController, address indexed newController); AFIMintCoordinator.MintCoordinated(bytes32 indexed signalId, uint256 indexed epochId, address indexed beneficiary, uint256 tokenAmount, uint256 receiptAmount); AFISignalReceipt.ReceiptMinted(address indexed to, uint256 indexed id, uint256 amount, bytes data). STRUCT: AFIMintCoordinator.MintRequest{address beneficiary; uint256 tokenAmount; uint256 receiptId; uint256 receiptAmount; bytes32 signalId; uint64 epoch; bytes extraData} — note this is calldata-only; signalId/epoch/extraData are emitted, never written to storage. ROLES: DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE (all three contracts), EMISSIONS_ROLE (AFIToken + AFIMintCoordinator), MINT_COORDINATOR_ROLE (AFISignalReceipt). CAP: TOTAL_SUPPLY_CAP = 86_000_000_000 * 1e18, enforced in mintEmissions. PERSISTED STATE: ERC-20 balances/totalSupply (XERC20 base), ERC-1155 balanceOf(to,id), AccessControl role maps, and the immutable token/receipts addresses on the coordinator. afi-mint/contracts/*.sol are empty stubs and afi-xerc20 is an out-of-scope vendored bridge fork, so they store no AFI commitment data.
answers.evidence: afi-token/src/AFIToken.sol:40; afi-token/src/AFIToken.sol:45; afi-token/src/AFIToken.sol:50; afi-token/src/AFIToken.sol:32; afi-token/src/AFIToken.sol:35; afi-token/src/AFIMintCoordinator.sol:19-27; afi-token/src/AFIMintCoordinator.sol:38-44; afi-token/src/AFIMintCoordinator.sol:14; afi-token/src/AFIMintCoordinator.sol:16-17; afi-token/src/AFISignalReceipt.sol:13; afi-token/src/AFISignalReceipt.sol:15; afi-mint/contracts/MintManager.sol:4-5; afi-xerc20/package.json:6

answers.question: C2: What do the docs claim is stored/guaranteed on-chain versus what the contracts actually encode?
answers.answer: The afi-token README markets the chain layer as fully decentralized and replayable — README.md:225 '✅ No centralized control' and README.md:227 '✅ Codex replayable & auditable', plus 'canonical chain' framing (README.md:63) — and per-repo docs claim every emissions mint is logged with novelty score and challenge history. The contracts encode far less: emissions can be minted by anyone holding EMISSIONS_ROLE, and the mainnet Pattern A deploy assigns BOTH DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE and EMISSIONS_ROLE to one Treasury Safe (DeployAFITokenMainnet.s.sol), which can also grant/revoke every role and mint up to the 86B cap — i.e. centralized control, not 'no centralized control'. Nothing in the contracts records novelty/score/challenge/replay data: the only on-chain provenance is the MintCoordinated event carrying signalId/epoch/amounts, with no payload, no score, and no hash. So 'codex replayable & auditable' is a doc claim backed only by off-chain afi-mint/afi-reactor systems, not by the on-chain ledger.

answers.question: C3: What does a third party need to verify a mint from chain data + published rules, and can they?
answers.answer: From chain data a third party can read MintCoordinated(signalId, epochId, beneficiary, tokenAmount, receiptAmount) and the paired EmissionsMinted/ReceiptMinted events, confirm the recipient's balance changed, and confirm totalSupply() stayed <= TOTAL_SUPPLY_CAP. That is the full extent of independent verification. They CANNOT verify, from chain data + published rules alone: (a) that the tokenAmount matches the goldpaper emissions formula — no emissions math exists on-chain (it is off-chain in afi-mint EmissionsMintDataProvider); (b) that signalId corresponds to real, scored off-chain evidence — no content/payload hash binds the receipt to anything; (c) that no governance vote overrode the deterministic validator decision — that logic is entirely off-chain. The sole on-chain gate is onlyRole(EMISSIONS_ROLE) on mintEmissions, so verification collapses to fully trusting whoever holds EMISSIONS_ROLE (the Treasury Safe / emissions agent). Therefore a mint's legitimacy is NOT independently verifiable from on-chain data plus published rules; the chain proves only that an authorized role minted within the cap.

answers.question: C4: The anchor gap — what are the current on-chain breadcrumbs versus the intended immutable commitment layer, and what is missing?
answers.answer: CURRENT (breadcrumb): the on-chain anchor for a mint is the MintCoordinated event with indexed signalId (bytes32), indexed epochId, indexed beneficiary, and the two uint amounts; the ERC-1155 receipt carries an opaque receiptId and a `data`/extraData blob that the reference coordinator passes as '0x'. signalId/epoch/extraData are emitted in logs only — never persisted to contract storage — so the provenance link is recoverable only by an event indexer, not from contract state. INTENDED (immutable commitment layer): docs imply codex-replayable provenance binding each mint to its signal evidence and scoring. MISSING ON-CHAIN: a content/payload hash binding the receipt to off-chain evidence (grep for contentHash|payloadHash|merkle|rulesetVersion in afi-token/src returns 0 hits — the only keccak256 uses are role-name constants), a pinned ruleset/transform version, UWR/score axes, and any persisted (non-event) provenance record. The receipt URI is still a TODO placeholder in the mainnet deploy script and there is no canonical per-receipt metadata schema. The gap is the absence of any cryptographic binding between the on-chain receipt and the off-chain evidence/scoring that justified the mint.

findings.title: No content/payload hash or ruleset-version anchored on-chain — receipts cannot be cryptographically bound to off-chain evidence
findings.evidence: afi-token/src/AFIMintCoordinator.sol:19-27 'struct MintRequest { address beneficiary; uint256 tokenAmount; uint256 receiptId; uint256 receiptAmount; bytes32 signalId; uint64 epoch; bytes extraData; }' (no hash field); grep -rniE 'contentHash|payloadHash|merkle|rulesetVersion' afi-token/src => 0 hits
findings.recommendation: Add a contentHash/payloadHash (and rulesetVersion) field to the receipt/MintRequest anchor so a mint can be cryptographically bound to its off-chain evidence; carry this into AFI_ONCHAIN_ANCHOR_GAP_ANALYSIS.md as the headline anchor gap.

findings.title: Mint legitimacy gated only by EMISSIONS_ROLE; on-chain enforces no scoring/finality, so an external validator must fully trust the role holder
findings.evidence: afi-token/src/AFIToken.sol:92 'function mintEmissions(address beneficiary, uint256 amount) external onlyRole(EMISSIONS_ROLE)'; afi-token/script/DeployAFITokenMainnet.s.sol:62-63 'address admin_ = treasurySafe; address emissionsController_ = treasurySafe;'
findings.recommendation: Document explicitly in the validator/replay spec that on-chain data cannot attest scoring legitimacy; finality/determinism is enforced off-chain by afi-mint before EMISSIONS_ROLE mints. State the trust assumption (Treasury Safe) clearly so external validators do not assume on-chain verifiability.

findings.title: signalId/epoch/extraData are emitted in event logs only, never persisted to contract storage
findings.evidence: afi-token/src/AFIMintCoordinator.sol:85 'emit MintCoordinated(req.signalId, req.epoch, req.beneficiary, req.tokenAmount, req.receiptAmount);' with MintRequest passed as calldata per afi-token/src/AFIMintCoordinator.sol:19-27; no storage mapping persists signalId/epoch
findings.recommendation: Note that the provenance link depends entirely on an event indexer; if logs are pruned/missed the signal->mint binding is unrecoverable from contract state. Consider persisting a minimal anchor mapping or clearly documenting the log-only provenance assumption.

findings.title: Single-beneficiary mint model — no split/gauge/multi-payee distribution on-chain
findings.evidence: afi-token/src/AFIMintCoordinator.sol:76 'token.mintEmissions(req.beneficiary, req.tokenAmount);' mints the full amount to a single beneficiary; MintRequest has exactly one beneficiary field at afi-token/src/AFIMintCoordinator.sol:20
findings.recommendation: Mark the single-beneficiary mint as a reference-impl decision; if econ splits/gauges are part of the protocol vision, specify them as a normative extension rather than implying they are already on-chain.

findings.title: ERC-1155 receipt has a placeholder URI and no canonical per-receipt provenance metadata schema
findings.evidence: afi-token/script/DeployAFITokenMainnet.s.sol:103 '"https://afi.protocol/receipts/{id}.json", // TODO: Update to production URI'; AFISignalReceipt mints with an opaque uint256 id and raw bytes data per afi-token/src/AFISignalReceipt.sol:35-44
findings.recommendation: Define a canonical receipt metadata schema (with content-hash linkage) in afi-config and resolve the URI before mainnet so receipts carry verifiable provenance rather than an opaque id.

findings.title: Off-chain mint_receipt_schema.json diverges from the on-chain mint anchor shape
findings.evidence: afi-mint/codex/mint_receipt_schema.json:6-13 required ['signal_id','score','mint_amount','validator_id','challenge_status','timestamp'] vs on-chain afi-token/src/AFIMintCoordinator.sol:19-27 (signalId, epoch, beneficiary, tokenAmount, receiptId, receiptAmount) — the two 'receipt' shapes share only signal_id/amount and disagree on epoch, beneficiary, score, and validator_id
findings.recommendation: Reconcile the off-chain codex receipt schema with the on-chain MintRequest/MintCoordinated anchor; pick one canonical receipt dialect and add the missing determinism fields (epoch, beneficiary, rulesetVersion, payloadHash) to both.

findings.title: 86B hard supply cap is enforced on-chain as an immutable constant
findings.evidence: afi-token/src/AFIToken.sol:35 'uint256 public constant TOTAL_SUPPLY_CAP = 86_000_000_000 * 1e18;'; afi-token/src/AFIToken.sol:97-98 'if (totalSupply() + amount > TOTAL_SUPPLY_CAP) { revert CapExceeded(); }'
findings.recommendation: Record the 86B cap as a normative commitment-plane invariant in the protocol surface spec; it is the one strong, independently-verifiable on-chain guarantee.

findings.title: afi-mint on-chain contracts are empty stubs; afi-xerc20 is an out-of-scope vendored bridge fork — the AFI commitment plane is solely afi-token
findings.evidence: afi-mint/contracts/MintManager.sol:4-5 'contract MintManager { // Stub for minting logic }'; afi-xerc20/package.json:6 '"homepage": "https://github.com/defi-wonderland/xERC20#readme"'
findings.recommendation: Document that afi-mint/contracts and afi-xerc20 hold no AFI commitment surface so readers do not mistake bridge-factory or stub deployments for the commitment plane.

contradictions.doc_says: README markets the on-chain layer as fully decentralized and codex-replayable: '✅ No centralized control' and '✅ Codex replayable & auditable'.
contradictions.code_does: Mainnet Pattern A grants BOTH DEFAULT_ADMIN_ROLE and EMISSIONS_ROLE to a single Treasury Safe that can mint to the 86B cap and grant/revoke all roles; the contracts anchor no score/novelty/replay data, only a breadcrumb event.

contradictions.doc_says: Docs frame BASE as the canonical chain holding a replayable/auditable provenance ledger of every emissions mint.
contradictions.code_does: On-chain provenance is a minimal event-log breadcrumb (signalId/epochId/beneficiary/amounts) with no content/payload hash, no scoring, and no persisted provenance record; replay determinism lives entirely off-chain.

contradictions.doc_says: Broader econ/reward vision implies multi-payee emission distribution per signal.
contradictions.code_does: MintCoordinator.mintForSignal mints the full tokenAmount to one req.beneficiary with no split/gauge logic anywhere in the contracts.

contradictions.doc_says: afi-mint codex publishes a MintReceipt schema (signal_id, score, mint_amount, validator_id, challenge_status, timestamp) as the audit/replay anchor.
contradictions.code_does: The on-chain anchor (MintRequest/MintCoordinated) uses a different field set (signalId, epoch, beneficiary, tokenAmount, receiptId, receiptAmount) with no score/validator_id, so the published receipt cannot be reconstructed from chain data.

contradictions.doc_says: afi-mint README advertises 'Solidity Contracts for On-Chain Mint Management' (MintManager/ThresholdRules/ChallengeRegistry).
contradictions.code_does: All three afi-mint contracts are empty stubs; the real commitment plane (caps, mint events, receipts) lives only in afi-token.
